title: Security
heroImageAlt: Aircraft maintenance engineer in high-visibility vest inspecting an open jet-engine cowling on the apron
intro: How we protect your booking, your payment information and your account.

sections.p:
All traffic to flyair.lk is served over HTTPS with TLS 1.2 or higher. That means everything you enter — your name, contact details, passport number, card number — is encrypted between your browser and our servers.

We use HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS) so your browser will refuse to talk to us over an unencrypted connection, even if asked to do so by a malicious link.

Card payments are processed by a regulated payment provider on a PCI-DSS compliant platform. We DO NOT store your full card number, the CVV or the expiry date on our own servers. Where saved cards are offered, only a non-reversible TOKEN representing the card is stored.

All card transactions go through 3-D Secure (OTP) verification, which means even if your card details were compromised elsewhere, an attacker would still need access to your OTP to charge it.

Admin access to customer data is restricted to a small number of trained staff. All admin sessions are time-limited and require two-factor authentication. Customer data is held in encrypted databases with regular backups.

Logs of every admin action — who viewed what, who changed what, who refunded what — are kept for accountability and review.

We use industry-standard protections against credential-stuffing, scraping and brute-force attempts. We deliberately do not disclose the exact systems — saying less makes our defences more effective — but they include rate limiting, CAPTCHA challenges on suspicious sessions, and continuous monitoring.

If you believe you have found a security vulnerability in flyair.lk, please email info@flyair.lk with "Security:" in the subject line. We will acknowledge within one working day and keep you informed while we investigate and remediate.

We ask that you do not publicly disclose a vulnerability until we have had a reasonable opportunity to fix it, and that you do not access, modify or destroy other people’s data while investigating.

sections.h: Phishing — how to verify it is really us
sections.p:
We will never ask you for your password, card CVV, or OTP by email, SMS, phone or WhatsApp. Genuine FlyAir emails come from @flyair.lk addresses; in case of doubt, type flyair.lk into your browser yourself rather than clicking a link.

If you receive a suspicious message claiming to be from FlyAir, forward it to info@flyair.lk and delete it.
